title: Constraint-satisfaction problems

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Constraint-satisfaction problems; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Artificial Intelligence; August 2023; Planning Systems, Constraint Satisfaction, Decision Making, and Reinforcement Learning foundations; atomic topic: Constraint-satisfaction problems
sources.claim: A constraint-satisfaction problem declares variables, finite domains, and constraints; propagation removes unsupported values and backtracking explores remaining assignments with reversible state.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A constraint-satisfaction problem declares variables, finite domains, and constraints; propagation removes unsupported values and backtracking explores remaining assignments with reversible state.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A map-coloring solver chooses an unassigned region, removes neighbor colors, backtracks on an empty domain, and validates every edge in the final assignment. Your goal is to implement and instrument a small CSP solver with a variable or value heuristic. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: State-space and adversarial search; Knowledge representation and uncertainty; dsa backtracking search; Logical inference and consistency. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: A constraint-satisfaction problem declares variables, finite domains, and constraints; propagation removes unsupported values and backtracking explores remaining assignments with reversible state. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Local consistency does not guarantee a solution, heuristics change work not solution validity, copying/restoration errors corrupt search, global constraints can propagate more strongly, and unsatisfiable needs evidence. The learner must reject this overclaim: If arc or forward consistency leaves every domain nonempty, a complete satisfying assignment is guaranteed.

includes: Model variables, domains and constraints and use propagation plus backtracking.
Implement and instrument a small CSP solver with a variable or value heuristic.
excludes: Local consistency does not guarantee a solution, heuristics change work not solution validity, copying/restoration errors corrupt search, global constraints can propagate more strongly, and unsatisfiable needs evidence.

outcomes: Implement and instrument a small CSP solver with a variable or value heuristic.

examples.title: Constraint-satisfaction problems: bounded direct case
examples.situation: A map-coloring solver chooses an unassigned region, removes neighbor colors, backtracks on an empty domain, and validates every edge in the final assignment.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A map-coloring solver chooses an unassigned region, removes neighbor colors, backtracks on an empty domain, and validates every edge in the final assignment.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Use propagation to prune unsupported values, then retain complete backtracking and validate every constraint on a returned assignment.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Use propagation to prune unsupported values, then retain complete backtracking and validate every constraint on a returned assignment.

examples.title: Constraint-satisfaction problems: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: Minimum-remaining-values reduces explored nodes on one puzzle but is reported as a heuristic rather than a completeness requirement.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: Minimum-remaining-values reduces explored nodes on one puzzle but is reported as a heuristic rather than a completeness requirement.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Local consistency does not guarantee a solution, heuristics change work not solution validity, copying/restoration errors corrupt search, global constraints can propagate more strongly, and unsatisfiable needs evidence.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: If arc or forward consistency leaves every domain nonempty, a complete satisfying assignment is guaranteed.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement and instrument a small CSP solver with a variable or value heuristic.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: If arc or forward consistency leaves every domain nonempty, a complete satisfying assignment is guaranteed.

analogy.example: a bounded operations desk that checks action preconditions, resource constraints, uncertain outcomes, and proxy rewards before acting can introduce Constraint-satisfaction problems only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: planning, constraints, expected utility, and sequential decisions
limitations: real preferences, uncertainty, stakeholders, safety constraints, and dynamics exceed one plan or reward score
